title: Wedelia trilobata
intro: Wedelia trilobata, also known as the suncare or creeping zinnia, is a flowering plant that belongs to the daisy family (Asteraceae). This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine due to its numerous health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it effective in treating wounds, fever, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Wedelia trilobata include flavonoids, glycosides, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as topical applications such as ointments and creams.
taxonomy: Wedelia trilobata is classified within the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Wedelia, which is part of the tribe Heliantheae. The tribe Heliantheae is a group of flowering plants within the subfamily Helianthoideae. The subfamily Helianthoideae is a part of the family Asteraceae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants. This classification reflects the evolutionary relationships between Wedelia trilobata and other related species.
uses: Wedelia trilobata has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, including treating fever, cough, and respiratory issues. Its leaves and flowers are used in teas and infusions to alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. The plant is also known to possess anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which may help in wound healing and reducing infections. In traditional medicine, Wedelia trilobata is used to treat various digestive issues, such as diarrhea and indigestion. Further research is needed to fully understand the extent of its medicinal uses and potential benefits.
benefits: Wedelia trilobata, a plant commonly known as three-lobed wedelia or three-lobed spider flower, has been traditionally used in herbal medicine. It is often used to treat various health issues due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The plant is believed to have potential in reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and supporting the immune system. parts: Wedelia trilobata, also known as Sutherlandia, is used in traditional medicine for various purposes. The plant's roots, leaves, and stems are used to prepare remedies. preparations: Wedelia trilobata has been used in traditional herbal medicine, particularly in Mexican folk remedies, where it's believed to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The leaves of the plant are often used to prepare infusions, which are said to alleviate symptoms of colds, fever, and respiratory issues. side_effects: Wedelia trilobata, also known as Mexican daisy or butterfly weed, has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for various purposes. However, it is essential to note that the side effects of Wedelia trilobata have not been extensively documented, and more research is needed to understand its potential effects on human health.
